req.title: Submit leave request — variant 2
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

tests.name: naive_case

"""
Pytest tests for leave request submission requirements:
- Full-time employees can submit leave requests for 1–30 business days.
- Requests spanning more than 30 business days require hr-admin approval.
- The action must be idempotent under retry.
"""

import uuid
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs – replace with real imports from your application
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum, auto
from typing import Optional


class EmployeeType(Enum):
    FULL_TIME = auto()
    PART_TIME = auto()
    CONTRACTOR = auto()


class LeaveStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    PENDING_HR_APPROVAL = "PENDING_HR_APPROVAL"
    APPROVED = "APPROVED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"


class LeaveRequestError(Exception):
    """Base error for leave request failures."""


class InvalidDurationError(LeaveRequestError):
    """Raised when the requested duration is outside the allowed range."""


class UnauthorisedEmployeeTypeError(LeaveRequestError):
    """Raised when a non-full-time employee tries to submit a leave request."""


@dataclass
class Employee:
    employee_id: str
    employee_type: EmployeeType


@dataclass
class LeaveRequest:
    request_id: str
    employee_id: str
    business_days: int
    status: LeaveStatus = LeaveStatus.PENDING
    requires_hr_approval: bool = False

# Service under test stub
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    """
    Thin wrapper around persistence + approval routing.
    Replace the body with real application logic.
    """

    STANDARD_MIN_DAYS = 1

    def __init__(self, repository, approval_router):
        self._repo = repositor

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for leave request submission requirements:
- Full-time employees can submit leave requests for 1–30 business days.
- Requests spanning more than 30 business days require hr-admin approval.
- The action must be idempotent under retry.
"""

import uuid
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs – replace with real imports from your application
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum, auto
from typing import Optional


class EmployeeType(Enum):
    FULL_TIME = auto()
    PART_TIME = auto()
    CONTRACTOR = auto()


class LeaveStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    PENDING_HR_APPROVAL = "PENDING_HR_APPROVAL"
    APPROVED = "APPROVED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"


class LeaveRequestError(Exception):
    """Base error for leave request failures."""


class InvalidDurationError(LeaveRequestError):
    """Raised when the requested duration is outside the allowed range."""


class UnauthorisedEmployeeTypeError(LeaveRequestError):
    """Raised when a non-full-time employee tries to submit a leave request."""


@dataclass
class Employee:
    employee_id: str
    employee_type: EmployeeType


@dataclass
class LeaveRequest:
    request_id: str
    employee_id: str
    business_days: int
    status: LeaveStatus = LeaveStatus.PENDING
    requires_hr_approval: bool = False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test stub
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class LeaveRequestService:
    """
    Thin wrapper around persistence + approval routing.
    Replace the body with real application logic.
    """

    STANDARD_MAX_DAYS = 30
    STANDARD_MIN_DAYS = 1

    def __init__(self, repository, approval_router):
        self._repo = repository
        self._router = approval_router

    def submit(self, employee: Employee, business_days: int,
               idempotency_key: str) -> LeaveRequest:
        """
        Submit a leave request.

        Parameters
        ----------
        employee        : the requesting employee
        business_days   : length of the requested leave in business days
        idempotency_key : caller-supplied unique key; a second call with the
                          same key returns the original result unchanged.

        Returns
        -------
        LeaveRequest
        """
        # --- idempotency check -------------------------------------------
        existing = self._repo.find_by_idempotency_key(idempotency_key)
        if existing is not None:
            return existing

        # --- authorisation ---------------------------------------------------
        if employee.employee_type != EmployeeType.FULL_TIME:
            raise UnauthorisedEmployeeTypeError(
                f"Only full-time employees may submit leave requests; "
                f"employee {employee.employee_id} is {employee.employee_type.name}."
            )

        # --- duration validation ---------------------------------------------
        if business_days < self.STANDARD_MIN_DAYS:
            raise InvalidDurationError(
                f"Leave duration must be at least {self.STANDARD_MIN_DAYS} "
                f"business day(s); got {business_days}."
            )

        # --- routing ---------------------------------------------------------
        requires_hr = business_days > self.STANDARD_MAX_DAYS
        status = (
            LeaveStatus.PENDING_HR_APPROVAL if requires_hr else LeaveStatus.PENDING
        )

        request = LeaveRequest(
            request_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
            employee_id=employee.employee_id,
            business_days=business_days,
            status=status,
            requires_hr_approval=requires_hr,
        )

        self._repo.save(reque
